title: 0DTE theta cost is always zero
description: For scalp preset: max_hold_days=0 and dte=0, so hold_days_effective = min(0, 0) = 0. theta_cost = abs(theta) * 0 * theta_accel = 0. The EV formula completely ignores theta decay for 0DTE options, producing wildly inflated EVs (1000%+).
evidence: Runtime log: EV=1246.2% on $0.01 CALL $692 when SPY=$679. hold_days_effective=min(0,0)=0 from config max_hold_days=0 and dte=0.
impact: Bot selects deeply OTM penny options with unrealistic EVs. Only saved by liquidity filter rejecting 98% of these. If a penny option passes liquidity, bot enters a near-certain loss.

title: Orphaned model records — file_path points to nonexistent .joblib
description: Models 8b4987ee and 385f4ea1 have status='ready' but their .joblib files do not exist on disk. Only 171859fb exists. If profile model_id were ever updated to point at these, model loading would crash.
evidence: ls options-bot/models/ shows only 2 .joblib files. DB has 4 model records. Python check: os.path.exists() returns False for 8b4987ee and 385f4ea1 paths.
impact: Potential crash if stale model IDs are referenced. Data integrity issue.

title: EV filter bid-ask spread check is dead code
description: bid and ask are hardcoded to None (line 356-357). The spread filter (lines 359-374) can NEVER fire. All spread filtering is deferred to post-scan liquidity_filter, but that only checks the BEST contract, not all candidates. A high-spread contract could beat a low-spread one on raw EV.
evidence: Code: line 356 'bid = None', line 357 'ask = None'. The if-block at line 359 is unreachable.
impact: Spread cost is never factored into EV scoring within the scanner. The best-EV contract may have terrible liquidity.

title: Signal logs for entered trades have step_stopped_at=None
description: When a trade IS entered, _write_signal_log is called with entered=True but step_stopped_at is not set (defaults to None). DB confirms: all entered=1 rows have step_stopped_at=NULL.
evidence: DB query: SELECT step_stopped_at FROM signal_logs WHERE entered=1 — all return NULL. Code at line ~1580: self._write_signal_log(entered=True, trade_id=trade_id) — no step_stopped_at arg.
impact: Minor data quality issue — cannot easily query what step successful entries reached.

title: Fallback Greeks estimate gamma as constant and theta as rough approximation
description: When broker Greeks fail (very common for 0DTE per log evidence), gamma is set to 0.015 (ATM) or 0.005 (OTM) regardless of actual moneyness/expiry. Theta is estimated as -(underlying_price * 0.0007) which for SPY ~$680 = -$0.476/day. Real 0DTE theta can be 10-50x higher.
evidence: Runtime log shows 50 out of 50 scored candidates used fallback Greeks today. The fallback theta of $0.476 is far below real 0DTE theta.
impact: EV calculations use inaccurate Greeks, further inflating EVs for short-dated options.

title: Model health: live accuracy 47.4% vs training 62.7%
description: Scalp model shows 47.4% live accuracy (9/19 correct) — below random 50%. Training accuracy was 62.7%. Model may be overfit or market regime has changed.
evidence: DB system_state model_health row: rolling_accuracy=0.4737, correct_predictions=9, total_predictions=19. Training metrics: acc_all=0.6268.
impact: Bot is trading on a model that performs worse than coin flip in live conditions.

title: Two database files — data/ copy is empty
description: options-bot/data/options_bot.db exists but has 0 tables. The real DB is at options-bot/db/options_bot.db. Stale empty file could confuse debugging.
evidence: sqlite3 connect to data/options_bot.db returns empty table list. db/options_bot.db has all 8 tables.
impact: Confusion during debugging. No runtime impact since config.py points to db/ path.

title: Browser opens before backend starts
description: start_bot.bat opens localhost:8000 and localhost:8000/system BEFORE python main.py starts. The 3-second timeout is before the second URL, not before main.py. User sees connection refused in browser.
evidence: Code: line 4 'start http://localhost:8000' then line 6 'start http://localhost:8000/system' then line 7 'python main.py'. Browser opens before Python starts.
impact: Poor UX on startup. User must manually refresh browser.

title: Feedback queue samples never consumed
description: training_queue has 2 rows, both with consumed=0. No code path appears to consume these samples for incremental retraining.
evidence: DB: SELECT consumed FROM training_queue — both rows show consumed=0, consumed_at=NULL.
impact: Feedback loop is non-functional. Completed trade outcomes are logged but never used for model improvement.

title: Entry Greeks show theta=0.0 vega=0 iv=0 for live trade
description: Trade 8991d423 (today's SPY 666 PUT) has entry_greeks with theta=0.0, vega=0, iv=0. The Greeks fallback estimated delta but left other Greeks at zero/null.
evidence: DB trades table: entry_greeks={'delta': -0.1701, 'gamma': 0.015, 'theta': 0.0, 'vega': 0, 'iv': 0} for trade_id 8991d423.
impact: Portfolio risk calculations (delta limit check) work with partial data. Theta/vega exposure is invisible to risk management.

title: Feedback queue actual_return_pct uses option PnL% not underlying return
description: enqueue_completed_sample passes pnl_pct (option P&L %) as actual_return_pct. But the model predicts UNDERLYING return %. A +20% option gain might correspond to only +0.5% underlying move. This mismatch means feedback data cannot be used to validate model accuracy.
evidence: Code: actual_return_pct=pnl_pct at line ~992. pnl_pct is calculated from option prices (line 940-944), not underlying prices.
impact: Feedback queue data is semantically wrong — model predicts underlying % but feedback stores option %. Incremental retraining on this data would corrupt the model.
